what_it_does: This file is a contract entry in the UI-contract ledger that defines the `model` resource: how a Claude model selection is represented (alias/name, effort, thinking, fallback chain), what operations are exposed (`model.list`, `model.act`), their current build status, bindings to supervisor/CLI flags, verification states, error vocabulary, and native precedence rules.
observations: Model selection is the tuple (model alias-or-name, effort, thinking, fallback); there is no `model://` fabric address.
The company does not own a model registry; it inherits Claude Code's closed alias set and full model names.
The supervisor spawn currently passes no `--model`, `--effort`, or `--fallback-model`, so sessions run the host account default.
`model.list` is `planned`; the file provides a static alias table as an interim catalog.
`model.act` is partially built: spawn-time selection threads to `--model`/`--effort`/`--fallback-model`, but mid-session `/model` switching is `planned` and returns `model.not-exposed`.
Verification states are granular: `model-at-spawn` is live-verified, `effort-at-spawn` is probe-verified, `set-mid-session` is unverified.
A resumed session keeps its saved model regardless of current settings.
Fable5 content-flagged requests fall back to Opus automatically; in non-interactive mode a flagged request ends the turn with a refusal.
`thinking` is not a CLI flag; it is controlled via `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS` or per-model defaults.
Effort values floor to the active model's supported maximum.
standouts: The contract explicitly names the gap between planned spawn params and the current reality where the supervisor passes no model lever.
`model.act` is split between built spawn-time wiring and an honest `planned`/501 mid-session switch.
Verification is tracked with distinct labels (live-verified, probe-verified, unverified) and dated evidence.
Fable5 safety fallback is documented as expected routing rather than a contract failure.
concerns: The same operation (`model.act`) mixes `building` and `planned` statuses, which could confuse consumers about what is callable now.
The request schema is labeled `PLANNED shape` even though spawn-time `model.act` is already built.
The fallback semantics (turn-only, excludes auth/billing/rate-limit/request-size/transport errors) are buried in interaction semantics and easy to overlook.
Live verification evidence is attributed to a lead prove-batch and may not be reproducible from the file alone.
notes: This contract documents the model-selection levers the company layer intends to expose, honestly marking spawn-time wiring as built while mid-session switching and a live model catalog remain planned.
questions: Which exact supervisor code parses the `/spawn` body and threads it into CLI flags?
How does the supervisor handle an invalid model alias or unsupported effort value at runtime?
What is the precise precedence when both a per-invocation `model` and `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL` are present?
How is the `thinking` toggle intended to be set if it is not exposed as a CLI flag?
Who owns and when will the mid-session `/model` control surface be built?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to contract consumer-facing model levers; the content matches, and it also acts as a gap analysis showing which levers are not yet wired.
apparent_themes: Honest capability accounting (built vs planned vs unverified).
Native-lever passthrough rather than a company-owned model registry.
Session-centric lifecycle where model is a launch attribute.
Safety routing treated as expected behavior, not fault.
Verification discipline distinguishing unit-test, probe, and live evidence.
intention_signals: It is trying to give UI consumers a transparent, versioned reference for model and reasoning configuration, making clear what the supervisor implements today versus what remains native-only in Claude Code and how to observe the resolved model on a live session.
novelty: It treats missing functionality as a first-class contract feature, using explicit error codes (`model.not-exposed`) and verification states instead of pretending unbuilt surfaces are available.
contribution: A precise, dated record of which model-selection capabilities are wired through the supervisor spawn, which remain Claude Code native-only, the schema and error vocabulary for `model.act`/`model.list`, and the verification evidence attached to each capability.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a contract entry for the `model` resource that defines the representation, operations (`model.list`, `model.act`), bindings, verification status, and error vocabulary for selecting and switching Claude models in company sessions, while explicitly marking spawn-time wiring as built and mid-session/catalog features as planned.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to give UI consumers a transparent, versioned reference for model-selection levers and to name the gaps where the company spawn currently delegates to Claude Code native defaults.
